verdict_reasoning: IZUMO4 is a member of the IZUMO family, which is canonically associated with sperm surface proteins mediating gamete fusion (IZUMO1 is the prototypical member, a single-pass TM protein on sperm outer leaflet). IZUMO4 (C19orf36) is the least-characterized family member; the NCBI summary places it in the nucleus, but family-lineage membership in the IZUMO family is a strong signal for tissue-restricted surface biology. Other IZUMO members (IZUMO1-4) have been detected on sperm membranes in a germline/gamete-restricted context. If IZUMO4 shares this architecture—a TM or GPI-anchored outer-leaflet display restricted to sperm/germline cells—it would qualify as contextual/tissue_restricted_surface. The nuclear localization noted by NCBI may reflect a somatic-cell expression context distinct from germline display. Given the strong family-lineage prior for surface display in germline cells and the narrow restriction, `contextual`/`tissue_restricted_surface` is the safer call over `no`.
predicted_key_uncertainty: IZUMO4 is poorly characterized; the NCBI summary places it in the nucleus in somatic cells, and it is unclear whether it has a TM domain or germline surface pool like other IZUMO family members.
